_notes: All Ro16 fixtures verified via search 2026-07-04 (beIN/Sky schedule, ESPN/FIFA match pages). Kickoff times converted from published UK/ET times to UTC.

QF pairing confirmed: Match 97 (Jul 9, Gillette Stadium, Boston) = winner Par/Fra vs winner Can/Mor; Match 98 (Jul 10, Los Angeles) = winner Bra/Nor vs winner Mex/Eng; Matches 99+100 (Jul 11, Miami & Kansas City) host the bottom half — venue assignment between those two not yet verified, so qf-3/qf-4 venues stay TODO.
Post-game workflow: set status:'final', fill score (+penalties), one-line recap; replace tbd ids in later rounds.

matches.recap: Azzedine Ounahi scored twice and Soufiane Rahimi added a stoppage-time third as Morocco ended the co-hosts' run and cruised into the quarterfinals.
matches.storyline: A co-host playing the first knockout match in its men's World Cup history, against the team that broke Africa's semifinal barrier in 2022. Morocco arrive ranked 7th in the world — their highest ever — after once again knocking out a European giant on penalties (the Netherlands this time, Spain last time). Canada bring the noise, the legs, and nothing to lose.
things_to_watch: The flank duel: Canada's Alphonso Davies and Morocco's Achraf Hakimi are two of the fastest attacking fullbacks alive, and they'll be running straight at each other all game.
Morocco's patience. They sit deep, soak up pressure, and strike on the counter — if the game looks 'boring' for stretches, that's them executing the plan that beat the Netherlands.
If it's still level late: Morocco have now won World Cup penalty shootouts against Spain (2022) and the Netherlands (2026). Canada have never been in one.
h2h: This is the first World Cup meeting between Canada and Morocco.
Canada's 1-0 win over South Africa in the Round of 32 was the first men's World Cup knockout win in their history.
matches.stakes: The winner meets the Paraguay/France survivor in the July 9 quarterfinal in Boston — for Canada that would be uncharted territory; for Morocco, a repeat of 2022's run.

matches.storyline: The tournament's giant-killers against its most complete team. Paraguay just produced one of the great World Cup upsets, holding Germany 1-1 and beating them in a shootout. France, finalists in 2022 and champions in 2018, cruised past Sweden 3-0 with Kylian Mbappé scoring twice — he has six goals and is hunting the Golden Boot.
things_to_watch: Mbappé starting wide on the left. Watch him stand still, then explode past the right back the instant the ball moves — six goals already this tournament.
Paraguay's wall. They defended for their lives against Germany and dared them to break through. Expect the same: deep defending, hard tackles, and lightning counters through Julio Enciso.
The longer it stays 0-0 or level, the more it favors Paraguay — they've already won a shootout this week, and France lost the 2022 final in one.
h2h: France beat Paraguay 1-0 in the 1998 Round of 16 on Laurent Blanc's golden goal — the last time they met at a World Cup, on France's way to the title.
Paraguay's shootout win over Germany in the Round of 32 was called one of the all-time World Cup upsets.
matches.stakes: The winner goes to Boston for the July 9 quarterfinal against Canada or Morocco.

matches.storyline: The country that has won this tournament five times against a country playing its first World Cup since 1998 — and the last time Norway showed up, they beat Brazil. Erling Haaland, maybe the most feared goalscorer on earth, has five goals; Brazil counter with Vinícius Júnior and a front line that just outscored Japan in a thriller.
things_to_watch: Haaland vs Brazil's center backs. He barely touches the ball for long stretches, then scores anyway — count how few touches he needs to create two big chances.
Vinícius Júnior cutting in from the left with defenders backing off in fear. He's in the Golden Boot chase and Norway will likely double-team him.
Norway's directness: long balls over the top toward Haaland the moment they win possession. It's simple and brutally effective.
h2h: Norway beat Brazil 2-1 in the group stage at France 1998 — one of the most famous results in Norwegian sports history.
Brazil have never beaten Norway in a full international.
matches.stakes: The winner heads to Los Angeles for the July 10 quarterfinal against the Mexico/England survivor.

matches.storyline: A World Cup knockout night at the Estadio Azteca — the cathedral where Pelé won in 1970 and Maradona conjured the 'Hand of God' in 1986. Mexico have been stuck at this exact stage for decades; the entire country calls the elusive quarterfinal 'el quinto partido' (the fifth game). Now they get to chase it at home, against an England side that has Harry Kane in Golden Boot form.
things_to_watch: The altitude. Mexico City sits at 2,200m and visiting players feel it — watch England's midfield legs in the last 20 minutes.
Harry Kane dropping deep. England's striker (five goals) keeps wandering back to midfield to pass, then sprinting into the box to finish.
Mexico's 17-year-old Gilberto Mora getting the ball between England's lines — the teenager plays with a calm that makes 90,000 people believe.
h2h: England beat Mexico 2-0 in the 1966 group stage on their way to their only World Cup title.
Mexico's only two World Cup quarterfinals came as hosts, in 1970 and 1986 — both at the Azteca.
matches.stakes: The winner faces Brazil or Norway in the July 10 quarterfinal in Los Angeles. For Mexico, 'el quinto partido' at home would be a national moment; for England, it's the road they expect to be on.

matches.storyline: The Iberian derby, in a World Cup knockout round, for the first time ever. Spain — European champions, ranked No. 2, just off their first World Cup knockout win since lifting the 2010 trophy — against a Portugal side that keeps finding late drama: Cristiano Ronaldo's penalty and a 94th-minute Gonçalo Ramos winner sank Croatia. Ronaldo is 41, playing a record sixth World Cup, and this could be his last dance against the neighbor.
things_to_watch: Lamine Yamal, 18, on Spain's right wing against Portugal's veteran defense — the tournament's youngest superstar against its oldest one.
Spain's passing web. They'll try to make Portugal chase the ball for minutes at a time; count the passes before Portugal touch it.
Ronaldo in the box in the final 15 minutes. His legs go 90 minutes less easily now, but he's scored in every act of this tournament's drama so far.
h2h: Their last World Cup meeting was the unforgettable 3-3 in 2018, when Ronaldo scored a hat-trick against Spain.
Spain's 3-0 over Austria in the Round of 32 was their first World Cup knockout win since winning the 2010 final.
matches.stakes: The winner meets the USA/Belgium survivor in a July 11 quarterfinal. Spain are chasing a second star; Portugal have never even reached a World Cup final.

matches.storyline: Twelve years after Belgium ended the USA's run in extra time in Brazil, the hosts get their rematch at home. The Americans just won their first World Cup knockout game since 2002 — but lost striker Folarin Balogun to a controversial red card, so he's suspended tonight. Belgium's aging golden generation, led by 35-year-old Kevin De Bruyne, needed a two-goal comeback and a disputed late penalty to survive Senegal.
things_to_watch: Who leads the USA line without the suspended Balogun — and whether the hosts press Belgium's slow center backs into mistakes.
Jérémy Doku one-on-one against the USA fullbacks. Belgium's winger is the most dangerous dribbler left in the tournament.
Malik Tillman on any free kick within 30 yards — his strike against Bosnia was one of the goals of the round.
h2h: Belgium beat the USA 2-1 in extra time in the 2014 Round of 16, the night Tim Howard set a World Cup record with 15 saves.
The USA's 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina was their first World Cup knockout victory since 2002.
matches.stakes: The winner gets Spain or Portugal in a July 11 quarterfinal. The USA have reached a World Cup quarterfinal once in the modern era — 2002. Belgium's golden generation is running out of tournaments.

matches.storyline: Lionel Messi against Mohamed Salah — two of the most beloved players of their era, meeting in a World Cup knockout for the first time. Messi, 39, leads the Golden Boot with seven goals as defending champions Argentina chase back-to-back titles, though tiny Cape Verde just gave them a real scare. Egypt are in the Round of 16 for the first time ever after winning their first-ever knockout match on penalties.
things_to_watch: Messi walking. He strolls for 80 minutes, reading the game — then one burst, one pass or one free kick decides it. Seven goals this tournament.
Salah on the counterattack. Egypt will defend deep and launch him the second Argentina lose the ball; his running battle with Argentina's left side is the game inside the game.
Egypt's belief. They've already made history this week — watch how long they can keep the game slow, scrappy and level.
h2h: This is the first World Cup meeting between Argentina and Egypt.
Argentina needed extra time to beat Cape Verde 3-2 in the Round of 32; Egypt beat Australia 4-2 on penalties after a 1-1 draw for their first knockout win.
matches.stakes: The winner faces Switzerland or Colombia in a July 11 quarterfinal. Argentina are chasing the first repeat title since Brazil in 1962; Egypt are already deeper than they've ever been.

matches.storyline: Switzerland just won a World Cup knockout match for the first time in 88 years, dispatching Algeria 2-0 — now they're one game from their first quarterfinal since 1954. In their way: a Colombia side built around Luis Díaz's speed and James Rodríguez's craft, which ground out a 1-0 win over Ghana. Two teams who defend well and hate giving anything away; expect a tight one.
things_to_watch: Luis Díaz vs Switzerland's right side — Colombia's winger is their one player who can turn nothing into a goal in three seconds.
Granit Xhaka bossing the middle. Switzerland's captain touches the ball more than anyone; the game flows entirely through him.
Set pieces. Both teams are organized and stingy in open play — a corner or free kick may well decide who makes the quarterfinal.
h2h: Their only previous World Cup meeting came in the group stage at USA 1994.
Switzerland's win over Algeria was their first World Cup knockout victory since 1938; Colombia's best-ever run ended in the 2014 quarterfinals.
matches.stakes: The winner meets Argentina or Egypt in a July 11 quarterfinal — a first quarterfinal in 72 years for Switzerland, or a first semifinal ever within reach for Colombia.
